title: Causal latent degradation state with pit-stop resets
summary: Recent F1 modeling work treats tyre pace as a latent state that evolves within a stint and resets after a pit stop.
rationale: Recent F1 modeling work treats tyre pace as a latent state that evolves within a stint and resets after a pit stop. Predictive-maintenance literature also finds change-point features improve remaining-life prediction versus static degradation summaries.
implementation_hint: Build per-`Race_Year x Driver` causal state features reset on `PitStop==1`: EWMA level of `LapTime_Delta`, EWMA slope, short-vs-long trend spread, cumulative positive delta streaks, and a simple change-point/cliff flag when recent pace deterioration accelerates. Robustly clip safety-car/red-flag outliers before filtering.
expected_effect: Captures nonlinear tyre-cliff behavior that static `TyreLife` and `Cumulative_Degradation` miss; especially useful on ambiguous pre-stop laps.
risk: Lap-time shocks from traffic or weather can masquerade as tyre cliffs. Needs robust filtering and possibly race-regime gating.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
